relationships.evidence: We partnered with SkillsFuture Singapore, a Singapore government agency, to upskill SMEs and help strengthen their sustainability skills through the DBS SME Skills Booster programme.

relationships.target: The Community Chest of Hong Kong
relationships.evidence: Regular events and conversations with Businesses for Impact, community partners and social service agencies to understand the social landscape and how DBS can partner to create impact.
Connection with government bodies and associations, including Singapore Centre for Social Enterprise and Community Chest.

Our technology colleagues actively volunteered their skills and time to nurture young girls’ interest in STEM through volunteering programmes with schools and community partners like Daughters of Tomorrow and launching DBS Tech For Girls.
This movement has attracted over 1,300 participants and generated approximately 3 tonnes of food impact and various collaborations between DBSF and financial institutions.
We strive to actively collaborate with various sectors including the Hong Kong Judiciary to address community challenges effectively.

Our firm applies Singapore Standard on Quality Management 1 which requires the firm to design, implement and operate a system of quality management including policies or procedures regarding compliance with ethical requirements, professional standards and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
DBS is a member of the SRAC, which was jointly set up by Singapore's Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA) and the Singapore Exchange Regulation (SGX RegCo).
We supported The Conservancy Association (‘CA’) in the Country Parks Plantation Enrichment Programme (‘PEP’), which was launched by the Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department.

relationships.target: World Wide Fund for Nature Hong Kong
relationships.evidence: We continued to provide support to the corporate membership programmes of the World Wide Fund for Nature Hong Kong.
We continued to provide support to the corporate membership programmes of the Business Environment Council.

relationships.target: The Community Chest of Hong Kong
relationships.evidence: We are the Diamond Sponsor of Dress Casual Day for the 25th consecutive year.

pain_points.pain_point: Financial support for individuals living with dementia
pain_points.evidence: There are various welfare benefits and financial support that a person living with dementia may be eligible to receive. Please contact the Social Service Welfare Department, elderly service units or organisations with dementia services for further information.

pain_points.pain_point: Support for understanding dementia and available services
pain_points.evidence: Speak to the Hong Kong Alzheimer’s Disease Association or other organisations offering dementia services, who can provide information to help you understand dementia and what to expect, including practical advice and support about living with the condition.

pain_points.pain_point: Employee wellness concerns related to work-related stress, and rewards and recognition.
pain_points.evidence: Through the annual employee engagement survey and quarterly employee experience survey, the key areas of concern raised by employees are around wellbeing (work-related stress), and rewards and recognition.

A growing number of SMEs and social enterprises are open to adopt more sustainable business models, but are often held back by operational matters and may lack the bandwidth or resources to kickstart their transition journey.

pain_points.pain_point: Need for continuous improvement in data governance and management.
pain_points.evidence: To improve our data quality, we are continually enhancing our data governance, systems, and processes.

pain_points.pain_point: Underprivileged girls facing barriers to STEM education opportunities.
pain_points.evidence: Our technology colleagues actively volunteered their skills and time to nurture young girls’ interest in STEM through volunteering programmes.

pain_points.pain_point: DBS has faced challenges incorporating material ESG factors into their reporting framework.
pain_points.evidence: Given the circumstances of the engagement, we also performed the following: interviewed management and personnel in Group Procurement Services, Group Human Resources, and Corporate Real Estate Strategy & Administration in relation to the Identified Sustainability Information.

pain_points.stakeholder: The Community Chest of Hong Kong
pain_points.pain_point: Need for continuous support and funding for their charitable programmes.
pain_points.evidence: We are the Diamond Sponsor of Dress Casual Day for the 25th consecutive year.
